word_list.word: it is an ill wind that blows (no one|no body) any good
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ɐn ˈɪl wˈɪnd ðæt blˈoʊz nˈoʊwˈʌn nˈoʊ bˈɑːdi ˌɛni ɡˈʊd

senses.definition: said to suggest that there is potential for good or positive outcomes, even in the face of adversity

examples.x: The journalist knew that it is an ill wind that blows no one any good when a scandal exposed corruption in government and led to calls for reform.

word_list.word: all is not lost that is in peril
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɔːl ɪz nˌɑːt lˈɔst ðæt ɪz ɪn pˈɛɹəl

senses.definition: used to suggest that there is still hope for a situation or outcome that is at risk or in danger, emphasizing the importance of maintaining optimism and perseverance in difficult times

examples.x: Even though our company is struggling financially, all is not lost that is in peril - we still have a chance to turn things around with a new marketing strategy.

word_list.word: the better the day, the better the deed
word_list.pronunciation: ðə bˈɛɾɚ ðə dˈeɪ ðə bˈɛɾɚ ðə dˈiːd

senses.definition: used to suggest that a good day or positive mindset can serve as a driving force for individuals to perform better and achieve success in their endeavors

word_list.word: better to light a (single|) candle than to curse the darkness
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ tə lˈaɪt ɐ sˈɪŋɡəl kˈændəl ðɐn tə kˈɜːs ðə dˈɑːɹknəs

senses.definition: used to suggest taking positive action, even if it is small, is more effective and productive than simply complaining or criticizing a situation without doing anything to improve it

examples.x: Instead of criticizing our politicians for not doing enough to combat climate change, it's better to light a candle than to curse the darkness by taking small actions in our daily lives to reduce our carbon footprint.

word_list.word: the darkest hour is just before the dawn
word_list.pronunciation: ðə dˈɑːɹkəst ˈaɪʊɹ ɪz dʒˈʌst bɪfˌoːɹ ðə dˈɔːn

senses.definition: used to suggest that when things seem to be at their worst and most difficult, it is often a sign that a positive change or breakthrough is about to happen

examples.x: After months of job searching with no luck, I started to lose hope - but then I remembered that the darkest hour is just before the dawn - and sure enough, I landed my dream job soon after.

word_list.word: every cloud has a silver lining
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi klˈaʊd hɐz ɐ sˈɪlvɚ lˈaɪnɪŋ

senses.definition: used to imply that even in difficult or negative situations, there is often something positive or hopeful to be found

examples.x: Although my flight was delayed, every cloud has a silver lining - I was able to catch up on some work while waiting at the airport.

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi ˈiːtʃ dˈeɪ bɹˈɪŋz ɪts bɹˈɛd wɪð ɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that one should focus on the present moment and not let concerns about what might happen in the future cause unnecessary stress or anxiety

examples.x: I used to get really anxious about the future, but then I started reminding myself that every day brings its bread.

word_list.word: he that lives in hope dances to an ill tune
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt lˈɪvz ɪn hˈoʊp dˈænsᵻz tʊ ɐn ˈɪl tˈuːn

senses.definition: used to suggest that relying solely on hope without taking practical steps to achieve one's goals can lead to disappointment and negative outcomes

examples.x: We can't keep hoping that our problems will disappear without taking responsibility and working on solutions - he that lives in hope dances to an ill tune.

word_list.word: in the end things will mend

senses.definition: used to imply that, despite the difficulties and challenges that one may face, there is hope for a positive outcome in the future with patience and perseverance

examples.x: We may be going through a rough patch in our relationship, but in the end things will mend - as long as we communicate openly and honestly, we can work through our issues.

word_list.word: it is always (darkest|darker) before the dawn
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz dˈɑːɹkəst dˈɑːɹkɚ bɪfˌoːɹ ðə dˈɔːn

senses.definition: used to imply that things often appear at their worst just before a significant positive change or improvement occurs

examples.x: I remember feeling hopeless during my job search, but then I reminded myself that it is always darkest before the dawn.
